cards.front: What does 'porque' (one word, no accent) mean?
cards.back: because (conjunction) — introduces the reason for something.
cards.grammarNote: Porque is a subordinating conjunction. It joins two clauses: 'Estoy feliz porque me ascendieron.' The clause after porque uses a regular indicative verb.

cards.front: What does 'por qué' (two words, with accent) mean?
cards.back: why? — used in direct and indirect questions.
cards.grammarNote: Por qué always carries a written accent on the qué. It appears in questions (¿Por qué lloras?) and in embedded questions (No sé por qué llora).

cards.front: Why aren't you coming? — Because I'm tired.
cards.back: ¿Por qué no vienes? — Porque estoy cansado.
cards.grammarNote: The question uses por qué (two words, accent); the answer uses porque (one word, no accent). A quick trick: the question form has the accent.

cards.front: I'm happy because I got a promotion.
I feel bad because I'm very tired.
cards.back: Me siento mal porque estoy muy cansado.

cards.front: I don't know why he's angry.
cards.back: No sé por qué está enojado.
cards.grammarNote: In indirect (embedded) questions, por qué keeps its accent and two-word form, but there are no question marks: 'No sé por qué...' (I don't know why...).

cards.front: I'm nervous because I have an important meeting.
cards.back: Estoy nervioso porque tengo una reunión importante.

cards.grammarNote: Ya que (since / given that) is a neutral-to-formal alternative to porque. It tends to be used when the reason is already known or obvious to both speakers.

cards.front: Since I feel bad, I'm staying home.
cards.back: Como me siento mal, me quedo en casa.
cards.grammarNote: Como (since/as) as a causal connector always starts the sentence and comes before the result. It is more conversational than ya que.

cards.front: I don't understand the reason for his anger.
cards.back: No entiendo el porqué de su enojo.
cards.grammarNote: El porqué (one word with accent) is a noun meaning 'the reason' or 'the why'. It is much less common than porque or por qué.

cards.front: Why are you crying? — Because I'm very happy.
cards.back: ¿Por qué lloras? — Porque estoy muy feliz.
